text: CSRF forces authenticated users to execute unwanted actions on a web application. The attacker tricks the victim's browser into sending a request that the application treats as legitimate because it carries the user's session cookie. Prevention: anti-CSRF tokens, SameSite cookies, custom headers.
SameSite cookie attribute (2020+): `Strict` (never sent cross-site), `Lax` (default since Chrome 80, sent on top-level navigation GET), `None` (always sent, requires Secure).
